chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنْ غُسْلِ الْإِحْرَامِ وَ مَا لَا يُجْزِئُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 78 – What suffices from washing for the Ihraam and what does not suffice

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/252)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/252)
text: رَجُلٍ اغْتَسَلَ لِإِحْرَامِهِ ثُمَّ قَلَّمَ أَظْفَارَهُ قَالَ يَمْسَحُهَا بِالْمَاءِ وَ لَا يُعِيدُ الْغُسْلَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel Bin Darraj, from one of his companions,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws regarding a man who washes for his Ihraam, then clips his nails. Heasws said: ‘He should wipe them with the water, and he would not repeat the washing’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “About a man who takes Ghusl (bath) for Ihram then trims his fingernails, abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, has said, ‘He should wipe them with water and he is not required to take Ghusl (bath) again.’”
